question: If it is difficult to balance the jetski at a stand still, what should I do?
content:
Do not operate the watercraft if the total weight of operator, passenger, and cargo exceeds 160 kg (353 lb). If it is difficult to balance at a standstill, use this boarding sequence:
1. Have the passenger steady the watercraft while the operator boards; the operator pulls up onto the platform into a seated position, then balances the watercraft.<PIC>
2. Attach the lanyard to your left wrist, install the clip on the lanyard to the engine shut-off switch, start the engine, and keep it at idle.
3. Have the passenger pull up onto the platform, kneel down, and crawl onto the seat as the watercraft accelerates.<PIC>
4. Once both riders are seated and stable, accelerate to planing speed, then reduce the throttle to the desired running speed.<PIC>
ret:
"Do not operate the watercraft if the total weight of operator, passenger, and cargo exceeds 160 kg (353 lb). If it is difficult to balance at a standstill, use this boarding sequence:
1. Have the passenger steady the watercraft while the operator boards; the operator pulls up onto the platform into a seated position, then balances the watercraft.<PIC>
2. Attach the lanyard to your left wrist, install the clip on the lanyard to the engine shut-off switch, start the engine, and keep it at idle.
3. Have the passenger pull up onto the platform, kneel down, and crawl onto the seat as the watercraft accelerates.<PIC>
4. Once both riders are seated and stable, accelerate to planing speed, then reduce the throttle to the desired running speed.<PIC>",["jetski_06", "jetski_07", "jetski_08"]
notes: Grounded in the WaveRunner boarding-with-passenger standstill-balance note. Images show the special boarding sequence and correspond one-to-one with the three PIC placeholders.
